commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a cache-friendly `disableOAuth` runtime option, daemon protocol forwarding, CLI flags, docs, examples, and regression tests for headless OAuth suppression with pooling.

Reproducibility: yes. for the remaining blocker: source inspection shows proxy calls still fetch schemas with `runtime.listTools(serverName, { includeSchema: true })` before the disabled-OAuth call options are applied. The broader runtime pooling behavior is also covered by the PR's integration tests and posted demo output.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is one narrow, source-backed blocker that an automated repair can attempt, while final API acceptance remains a maintainer decision.

target.title: fix(runtime): preserve disableOAuth across headless paths
target.body:
## Summary

Headless MCPorter callers can now suppress interactive OAuth without losing the connection pooling they need for daemon and repeated-tool workflows. `disableOAuth` now stays intact across direct runtime helpers, keep-alive daemon paths, and CLI commands instead of only working at the low-level `connect()` entry point.

This closes openclaw/mcporter#197.

## What changed

- Preserves cached no-OAuth connections through `callTool`, `listTools`, `listResources`, and `readResource`, including the case where a caller pre-connects with `disableOAuth: true` and then uses higher-level helpers.
- Forwards the option through keep-alive runtime wrappers, daemon protocol/host handling, and daemon fast-path calls so daemon-managed servers cannot accidentally re-enable OAuth.
- Exposes `--disable-oauth` / `--no-oauth` for `mcporter call`, `mcporter list`, and `mcporter resource`.
- Normalizes `disableOAuth: false` to the same cache identity as omitting the option.
- Adds regression coverage for OAuth server suppression, 401 promotion suppression, helper reuse, daemon forwarding, CLI parsing, and tool metadata cache keys.

## Demo

A local terminal evidence capture was recorded at:

`/var/folders/l7/gpl83h991dbfbmbr1v8860f00000gn/T/mcporter-disable-oauth-demo-XXXXXX.KvYUH5Y2tf/disable-oauth-cli-evidence.svg`

It shows the actual built CLI help for `call`, `list`, and `resource` exposing `--disable-oauth`.

## Test plan

- `./runner pnpm run check`
- `./runner pnpm test`
- `./runner pnpm exec tsx scripts/docs-list.ts`
- `./git diff --check`

---

[![Compound Engineering](https://img.shields.io/badge/Built_with-Compound_Engineering-6366f1)](https://github.com/EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin)
![Pi](https://img.shields.io/badge/Pi_Coding_Agent-8B5CF6)
target.author: feniix

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

target.title: feat: add tart provider for macOS VMs on Apple Silicon
target.body:
## Summary

Add a `tart` provider for running macOS VMs on Apple Silicon via [Cirrus Labs tart](https://github.com/cirruslabs/tart). This enables local macOS staging environments using Apple's native Virtualization.framework -- no Parallels license needed.

## How it works

- **Acquire**: `tart clone` from OCI base image, `tart set` CPU/memory/disk, `tart run --no-graphics`, poll `tart ip`, inject SSH key via `tart exec`, wait for SSH readiness
- **Resolve**: find running VM by `crabbox-` name prefix
- **List**: `tart list --format json` filtered by prefix
- **Release**: `tart stop` + `tart delete`
- **Cleanup**: garbage collect stale crabbox-owned VMs past idle timeout
- **Doctor**: verify tart CLI and list capability
- **SSH key injection**: via `tart exec` (no sshpass, no credentials on argv)
- **Ready check**: macOS-safe `uname -s && test -d ~` (no crabbox-ready script needed)
- **Kept lease persistence**: `tart run` is detached via `Process.Release()` for `--keep` leases so the VM survives crabbox exit (matching how `docker run -d` delegates to the daemon)

## Crabbox CLI Proof

Real `crabbox warmup`, `crabbox run`, and `crabbox stop` on M4 Pro Mac (macOS 26.4.1), tart 2.32.1, latest head:

### crabbox warmup
```
$ crabbox warmup --provider tart --target macos --tart-image ghcr.io/cirruslabs/macos-ventura-base:latest
provisioning provider=tart lease=cbx_733710e11ba8 slug=crimson-prawn cpus=4 memory=8192MB disk=50GB keep=true
provisioned lease=cbx_733710e11ba8 instance=crabbox-crimson-prawn-df015648 state=ready
ready ssh=admin@192.168.64.2:22 workroot=/Users/admin/crabbox
warmup complete total=17.771s
```

### crabbox run (sync + command execution)
```
$ cd /tmp/crabbox-build && crabbox run --provider tart --target macos -- sw_vers
provisioning provider=tart lease=cbx_aff2eb72cbdb slug=golden-hermit cpus=4 memory=8192MB disk=50GB
provisioned lease=cbx_aff2eb72cbdb instance=crabbox-golden-hermit-9d05603f state=ready
sync candidate: 604 files, 7.5 MiB
sync complete in 3.582s
running on 192.168.64.2 sw_vers
ProductName:    macOS
ProductVersion: 13.7.4
BuildVersion:   22H420
command complete in 86ms total=3.678s
releasing cbx_aff2eb72cbdb
```

### crabbox stop
```
$ crabbox stop --provider tart --target macos --id cbx_733710e11ba8
released lease=cbx_733710e11ba8 instance=crabbox-crimson-prawn-df015648
```

### warmup --keep (VM persists after crabbox exit)

Fixed in 24da390 -- kept leases now survive crabbox exit:

```
$ bin/crabbox warmup --provider tart --target macos --tart-image ghcr.io/cirruslabs/macos-ventura-base:latest --keep
provisioning provider=tart lease=cbx_1ed67916ec86 slug=harbor-shrimp cpus=4 memory=8192MB disk=50GB keep=true
provisioned lease=cbx_1ed67916ec86 instance=crabbox-harbor-shrimp-68e78d88 state=ready
ready ssh=admin@192.168.64.2:22 network=public workroot=/Users/admin/crabbox
warmup complete total=17.253s

# crabbox has exited -- verify VM is still running:
$ tart list --format json
crabbox-harbor-shrimp-68e78d88  State=running  Running=true

$ bin/crabbox status --provider tart --target macos --id cbx_1ed67916ec86
cbx_1ed67916ec86 state=ready ready=true keep=true idle_for=18s

$ bin/crabbox run --provider tart --target macos --id cbx_1ed67916ec86 -- sw_vers
sync candidate: 604 files, 7.5 MiB
sync complete in 3.718s
ProductName:    macOS
ProductVersion: 13.7.4
BuildVersion:   22H420
command complete in 116ms total=3.855s

$ bin/crabbox stop --provider tart --target macos --id cbx_1ed67916ec86
released lease=cbx_1ed67916ec86 instance=crabbox-harbor-shrimp-68e78d88

$ tart list  # no crabbox VMs remaining after cleanup
```

## Configuration

CLI flags: `--tart-image`, `--tart-cpu`, `--tart-memory`, `--tart-disk`

YAML (`.crabbox.yaml`):
```yaml
tart:
  image: ghcr.io/cirruslabs/macos-ventura-base:latest
  user: admin
  cpus: 4
  memory: 8192
  disk: 50
```

Env vars: `CRABBOX_TART_IMAGE`, `CRABBOX_TART_USER`, `CRABBOX_TART_WORK_ROOT`, `CRABBOX_TART_CPUS`, `CRABBOX_TART_MEMORY`, `CRABBOX_TART_DISK`

## Not included (follow-up)

- Desktop/VNC (tart VMs run headless; Screen Sharing setup is a follow-up)
- Shared-directory mounts (`tart run --dir`; needs explicit host-mount config)
- Checkpoint/fork (tracked as a separate follow-up PR)
- `config show` tart section (follow-up)

## Files changed

- `internal/providers/tart/` -- provider, backend, core, flags, tests
- `internal/cli/config.go` -- TartConfig struct, file config, env var loading
- `internal/cli/providers_builtin_test.go` -- test provider registration
- `internal/providers/all/all.go` -- import registration
- `docs/providers/tart.md` -- provider documentation
- `docs/providers/README.md` -- provider table entry
- `docs/source-map.md` -- source map entry

## Test plan

- [x] 15 provider unit tests pass
- [x] `go build` / `go vet` / `go test` pass (Go 1.26.4 darwin/arm64)
- [x] Real `crabbox warmup --provider tart` (17.7s)
- [x] Real `crabbox warmup --keep` -- VM persists after crabbox exit
- [x] Real `crabbox run --provider tart -- sw_vers` (sync 3.6s + command 86ms)
- [x] Real `crabbox run` on kept lease (reuse existing VM)
- [x] Real `crabbox stop --provider tart`
- [x] Clean cleanup -- no crabbox VMs left after stop
- [x] SSH key injection via tart exec (no sshpass)
- [x] Resource flags applied before boot
- [x] YAML + env var config wired
- [x] Cleanup scoped to crabbox-owned VMs only
- [x] Provider docs + source map + provider index
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
